content.text: Automated Architecture Tests for GenAI Systems

Architectural fitness functions are automated tests that verify architectural properties of a codebase — properties like 'the business logic never directly imports the LLM SDK' or 'every LLM call has an OTel span'. They complement unit and integration tests by testing structure and contracts rather than behaviour.

For GenAI systems, architectural fitness functions are especially valuable because the properties they enforce — adapter isolation, prompt hygiene, observability contracts, resilience patterns, and cost governance — are invisible to functional tests. A unit test that mocks the LLM cannot tell you whether the adapter is correctly isolated. A load test cannot tell you whether every prompt template has a safety baseline. Fitness functions fill that gap.

attrs.alt: Five categories of architectural fitness functions for GenAI systems

content.text: What Architectural Fitness Functions Are

The term was introduced by Neal Ford, Rebecca Parsons, and Patrick Kua in Building Evolutionary Architectures (2017). A fitness function is any mechanism that provides an objective, automated assessment of some architectural characteristic. They are not a single technology — they can be:
Import graph tests:
parse the module dependency graph and assert that certain modules never import certain other modules
AST-based static analysis:
walk the abstract syntax tree of source files to detect forbidden patterns (hardcoded strings, missing error handling, direct SDK usage)
Schema and lint checks:
validate that configuration files (YAML prompt templates) conform to expected structure and content rules
Attribute-level unit tests:
call the adapter with a mock and assert that the OTel span it emits has the required attributes
Config-level assertions:
check that environment configuration (max_tokens, concurrency limits, alert definitions) satisfies invariants

Fitness functions are not a substitute for architectural discipline — they are the mechanism that makes discipline automatic. The goal is to make it impossible to accidentally violate an architectural property without CI failing.
Category 1: Adapter Isolation Tests

The most important architectural property of a GenAI system is that all LLM calls are made through a single adapter layer. The rest of the application — domain logic, service layer, API handlers — must never import the LLM SDK directly. This isolation makes the LLM backend swappable, the application unit-testable without API calls, and the call surface auditable.
Import Graph Fitness Function
Business Logic Layer Test
Category 2: Prompt Hygiene Tests

Prompt hygiene tests verify that prompt templates follow the team's authoring standards: stored as YAML (not inline strings), containing the safety baseline, using correct semantic versioning, and delimiting user input with XML tags.
No Inline Prompts Fitness Function
Category 3: Observability Contract Tests

Every LLM call must emit an OTel span with a defined set of attributes. An observability contract test calls the adapter with a mock Anthropic client and inspects the emitted span to verify the required attributes are present.

# tests/architecture/test_observability_contract.py
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from opentelemetry import trace
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export.in_memory_span_exporter import InMemorySpanExporter
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import SimpleSpanProcessor

from src.adapters.llm_adapter import LLMAdapter  # your adapter

REQUIRED_ATTRS = {
    "llm.provider",
    "llm.model",
    "llm.request.max_tokens",
    "llm.usage.input_tokens",
    "llm.usage.output_tokens",
}

@pytest.fixture
def tracer_with_exporter():
    exporter  = InMemorySpanExporter()
    provider  = TracerProvider()
    provider.add_span_processor(SimpleSpanProcessor(exporter))
    trace.set_tracer_provider(provider)
    return provider, exporter

def test_llm_adapter_emits_required_span_attributes(tracer_with_exporter):
    provider, exporter = tracer_with_exporter

    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.content = [MagicMock(text="Test response")]
    mock_response.stop_reason = "end_turn"
    mock_response.usage = MagicMock(input_tokens=42, output_tokens=17)
    mock_response.model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"

    with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") as mock_client_class:
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = mock_response
        mock_client_class.return_value = mock_client

        adapter = LLMAdapter(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", max_tokens=128)
        adapter.complete(system="You are a test assistant.", user_message="Hello")

    spans = exporter.get_finished_spans()
    assert spans, "No OTel spans were emitted — adapter must instrument its LLM calls"

    llm_span = next((s for s in spans if "llm" in s.name.lower()), spans[0])
    emitted_attrs = set(llm_span.attributes.keys())
    missing = REQUIRED_ATTRS - emitted_attrs

    assert not missing, (
        f"OTel span is missing required attributes: {missing}. "
        "See docs/observability-contract.md for the full required attribute list."
    )

def test_llm_span_has_no_pii_in_attributes(tracer_with_exporter):
    """User message content must not appear verbatim in span attributes."""
    provider, exporter = tracer_with_exporter

    pii_message = "My email is test@example.com and SSN is 123-45-6789"
    mock_response = MagicMock()
    mock_response.content = [MagicMock(text="Got it")]
    mock_response.stop_reason = "end_turn"
    mock_response.usage = MagicMock(input_tokens=10, output_tokens=5)
    mock_response.model = "claude-haiku-4-5-20251001"

    with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") as mock_client_class:
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = mock_response
        mock_client_class.return_value = mock_client

        adapter = LLMAdapter(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", max_tokens=64)
        adapter.complete(system="You are a test assistant.", user_message=pii_message)

    spans = exporter.get_finished_spans()
    for span in spans:
        for key, value in span.attributes.items():
            assert "test@example.com" not in str(value), (
                f"PII found in span attribute '{key}'. "
                "User message content must not be recorded in spans."
            )
Category 4: Resilience Contract Tests

Resilience tests verify that the adapter correctly retries on recoverable errors, respects the retry limit, sets a timeout, handles max_tokens truncation, and provides a fallback path. These are architectural properties that must hold regardless of what the application code does.

# tests/architecture/test_resilience_contract.py
import pytest, time
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from anthropic import RateLimitError, APIStatusError
from src.adapters.llm_adapter import LLMAdapter

def make_rate_limit_error():
    response = MagicMock()
    response.status_code = 429
    return RateLimitError("Rate limited", response=response, body={})

def test_adapter_retries_on_rate_limit():
    """Adapter must retry on 429, not raise immediately."""
    success_response = MagicMock()
    success_response.content = [MagicMock(text="success")]
    success_response.stop_reason = "end_turn"
    success_response.usage = MagicMock(input_tokens=5, output_tokens=3)

    with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") as mock_class:
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        # First two calls raise 429, third succeeds
        mock_client.messages.create.side_effect = [
            make_rate_limit_error(),
            make_rate_limit_error(),
            success_response,
        ]
        mock_class.return_value = mock_client

        adapter = LLMAdapter(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", max_tokens=64)
        result = adapter.complete(system="You are helpful.", user_message="Hi")

    assert result == "success"
    assert mock_client.messages.create.call_count == 3

def test_adapter_does_not_exceed_max_retries():
    """Adapter must stop after 4 retries and raise, not loop forever."""
    with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") as mock_class:
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_client.messages.create.side_effect = make_rate_limit_error()
        mock_class.return_value = mock_client

        adapter = LLMAdapter(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", max_tokens=64)
        with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
            adapter.complete(system="You are helpful.", user_message="Hi")

    assert mock_client.messages.create.call_count <= 5  # max 4 retries + 1 initial

def test_adapter_handles_max_tokens_stop_reason():
    """Adapter must not silently pass through truncated output."""
    truncated = MagicMock()
    truncated.content = [MagicMock(text="This response was cut off mid-sen")]
    truncated.stop_reason = "max_tokens"
    truncated.usage = MagicMock(input_tokens=10, output_tokens=64)

    with patch("anthropic.Anthropic") as mock_class:
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = truncated
        mock_class.return_value = mock_client

        adapter = LLMAdapter(model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001", max_tokens=64)
        result, metadata = adapter.complete_with_metadata(
            system="You are helpful.", user_message="Tell me a very long story."
        )

    assert metadata["stop_reason"] == "max_tokens"
    # Adapter must signal truncation — caller decides whether to use truncated output
    assert metadata.get("truncated") is True, (
        "Adapter must set metadata['truncated'] = True when stop_reason == 'max_tokens'. "
        "Callers must be able to detect truncation."
    )
Category 5: Cost Governance Tests
Cost governance tests verify configuration invariants: max_tokens is set on every LLM call, batch jobs have a concurrency cap, and the cost alert is configured in the alerting definition file.
Running Fitness Functions in CI

Fitness function tests run as part of the standard pytest suite. They are fast (< 30 seconds total), require no API calls (they test structure, not behaviour), and run on every PR alongside unit tests.

# .github/workflows/ci-genai.yml (fitness function job)
fitness-functions:
  name: "Architectural Fitness Functions"
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
      with: { python-version: "3.12" }
    - run: pip install pytest pyyaml opentelemetry-sdk anthropic
    - name: Run fitness functions
      run: pytest tests/architecture/ -v --tb=short
      # No API key needed — all LLM calls are mocked in architecture tests
Fitness Function Test Report
Configure pytest to produce a structured report that maps failures to the architectural property they violated:

# conftest.py — add architectural category markers
import pytest

def pytest_configure(config):
    config.addinivalue_line("markers", "adapter_isolation: tests for adapter isolation property")
    config.addinivalue_line("markers", "prompt_hygiene: tests for prompt hygiene property")
    config.addinivalue_line("markers", "observability: tests for observability contract")
    config.addinivalue_line("markers", "resilience: tests for resilience contract")
    config.addinivalue_line("markers", "cost_governance: tests for cost governance")

# Run: pytest tests/architecture/ -v --tb=short -m adapter_isolation
# to run only adapter isolation fitness functions
Evolving Fitness Functions with the Architecture

Fitness functions must be maintained as the architecture evolves. An outdated fitness function that allows a pattern the team has decided to forbid is worse than no fitness function — it creates a false sense of safety.
When to Add a New Fitness Function
After a post-incident:

If an incident reveals that an architectural property was violated (e.g., a new service made a direct LLM call without going through the adapter), add a fitness function to prevent the same class of violation from recurring
When adopting a new architectural pattern:

If the team decides that all new LLM features must use structured output (JSON mode), add a fitness function that verifies structured output is configured when the prompt's expected_output is not plain text
When the governance policy changes:
If the safety baseline is updated to v3.0, update the fitness function that checks the minimum version
When a technology boundary is established:
If the team decides that only the ML platform team's approved adapters may call the LLM API, add a fitness function that verifies no other package calls it
Fitness Function Maintenance Table
Fitness function
Review trigger
Owner
Adapter isolation
New service or module added to the codebase
Architecture chapter
Safety baseline version check
Safety baseline version updated in governance policy
ML platform team
OTel required attributes
Observability contract updated (new required attribute added)
Platform observability team
Resilience: max retries
Retry policy updated in engineering standards
ML platform team
max_tokens required
Cost governance policy updated
Engineering manager
Summary
Architectural fitness functions are automated tests that verify structural properties of a GenAI codebase — properties that functional tests cannot catch
Five categories of fitness functions cover the most important GenAI architectural properties: adapter isolation, prompt hygiene, observability contracts, resilience contracts, and cost governance
Fitness functions run in CI alongside unit tests; they require no API calls and complete in under 30 seconds
The adapter isolation test (no direct SDK imports outside the adapter layer) is the most foundational — it enforces the single point of control for all LLM calls
Observability contract tests use an in-memory OTel span exporter to verify that every LLM call emits the required attributes without making real API calls
Fitness functions must be maintained as the architecture evolves; after every incident or policy change, update or add the fitness function that would have caught the violation
